data.title: Global OTC drugs market expected to reach USD 242.94 billion by 2034, up from USD 135.25 billion in 2025, with a 6.72% CAGR growth. Major markets: North America - USD 39.77 billion in 2024, Europe - USD 27.71 billion in 2024, Asia Pacific - USD 52.51 billion in 2024.
data.description: The global over the counter (OTC) drugs market is projected to be worth over USD 242.94 billion by 2034, increasing from USD 135.25 billion in 2025 with a strong CAGR of 6.72% from 2025 to 2034. Factors contributing to this growth include the expansion of e-commerce, rising popularity of self-medication, and increasing healthcare expenditure. OTC drugs are medications sold without a prescription, used to treat minor conditions like colds, headaches, and allergies. They are available in various formats like tablets and ointments and are sold in convenience stores, pharmacies, and online. In 2024, the North America market exceeded USD 39.77 billion, Europe accounted for USD 27.71 billion, and Asia Pacific reached USD 52.51 billion. The oral segment held a market share of 56.66% in 2024, while the parenteral segment had a 20.24% market share. Key products contributing to market size include vitamin and dietary supplements, cough and cold products, analgesics, and gastrointestinal products.

data.title: AGF Investments declares August 2025 cash distributions for AGF Enhanced U.S. Equity Income Fund, AGF Total Return Bond Fund, and AGF Systematic Global Infrastructure ETF. Record date: August 29, 2025; payout on September 5, 2025. TSX:AGF.B.
data.description: On August 22, 2025, AGF Investments Inc. shared updates regarding the August 2025 cash distributions for a few of its funds. The AGF Enhanced U.S. Equity Income Fund, AGF Total Return Bond Fund, and AGF Systematic Global Infrastructure ETF, all of which offer monthly distributions, were specifically highlighted. Cash distributions will be made to unitholders of record on August 29, 2025, with the payments scheduled for September 5, 2025. These distributions are of significance to investors in TSX-listed AGF Investments, with the announcement aimed at providing clarity and transparency regarding the upcoming payments for the mentioned funds.

data.title: Americas Gold and Silver finds high-grade 149 Vein extension - 24,913 g/t Silver and 16.9% Copper; share consolidation completed.
data.description: Americas Gold and Silver Corporation, a prominent North American precious metals producer trading under the symbols TSX: USA and NYSE American: USAS, has released positive exploration findings at the Galena Complex. Interestingly, these results reveal the discovery of a high-grade upper extension of the 149 Vein. In addition to this exciting news, the Company has successfully implemented a share consolidation as previously disclosed. Following this consolidation, trading is anticipated to occur on a post-consolidation basis.

data.title: Linn Andersson appointed EVP Strategy and CTO of Boliden, responsible for strategy, business, tech dev.
data.description: In recent news, Linn Andersson has been named the Executive Vice President of Strategy and Chief Technology Officer of Boliden. This newly established position within Boliden's Group Management puts Andersson in charge of strategy, business, and technology development, along with procurement. With this appointment, Andersson will have a crucial role overseeing various key aspects of the company's operations.

data.title: China Automotive Industry Outlook Report for 2025 highlights key drivers, challenges, and growth opportunities, focusing on passenger vehicles, including vans, SUVs, MPVs, and cross vehicles. The report also covers China's electric vehicle sector, with emphasis on charging and battery-swapping infrastructure.
data.description: A recently released report titled "China Automotive Outlook, 2025" is now accessible through ResearchAndMarkets.com. The report delves into the automotive sector of China, particularly emphasizing passenger vehicles (PVs) like vans, sports utility vehicles (SUVs), multipurpose vehicles (MPVs), and cross vehicles. Additionally, the study evaluates China's electric vehicle (EV) domain, encompassing aspects such as the charging infrastructure and battery-swapping facilities. The report sheds light on the latest developments and projections within China's automotive industry as it heads towards 2025.

data.title: Ubiquiti Inc. reports strong Q4 revenue of $759.2 million, full fiscal year revenue reaches $2.6 billion in 2025.
data.description: Ubiquiti Inc. (NYSE: UI) revealed its financial performance for the fourth quarter and full year fiscal 2025, ending on June 30, 2025. In the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025, the company reported revenues amounting to $759.2 million. The GAAP diluted earnings per share (EPS) stood at $4.41, while the non-GAAP diluted EPS was $3.54. For the entire fiscal 2025, Ubiquiti recorded revenues totaling $2.6 billion, with a GAAP diluted EPS of $11.76 and a non-GAAP diluted EPS of $10.96. Additionally, the Board of Directors also shared that...

data.title: Chime (NASDAQ: CHYM) partners with Workday (NASDAQ: WDAY) to offer financial wellness benefits, focusing on employees.
data.description: In San Francisco, Chime® (NASDAQ: CHYM) has revealed a crucial partnership with Workday (NASDAQ: WDAY) as a Workday Wellness partner for financial benefits. This collaboration aims to combine Chime Workplace™, offering a comprehensive range of financial wellness tools, with Workday Wellness to prioritize financial well-being as a key aspect of employee benefits. Workday Wellness, an AI-driven program, is set to transform employee financial wellness by integrating Chime's solutions.

data.title: Investors await Jerome Powell's speech at Jackson Hole as Fed's September rate cut chances drop to 73%. Bitcoin at $113,000 and Ethereum at $4,280, while total crypto market cap hits $3.83 trillion. Flows indicate investors hedging before Powell's remarks.
data.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's upcoming speech at Jackson Hole has caught the attention of global markets. Despite pressure from President Trump, the Fed has kept borrowing costs unchanged over five meetings. The likelihood of a rate cut in September has decreased from over 85% to around 73% according to CME FedWatch. Concerns over stalled disinflation and tariffs have influenced this shift. Powell's speech holds weight as it will be his last Jackson Hole keynote before his term ends in 2026. In the crypto markets, Bitcoin dipped to nearly $113,000 and Ethereum held at $4,280. ETF flows turned negative recently, indicating investors may be hedging ahead of Powell's address. The total crypto market cap stands at about $3.83 trillion. This news can be read in full on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Gulf & Pacific Equities reports Q2 revenue of $1,124,742, down 1.7% from last year and welcomes La Vie en Rose to Tri-City Mall.
data.description: Toronto, August 22, 2025 - Gulf & Pacific Equities Corp., identified as TSX-V: GUF, a renowned company concentrating on obtaining, overseeing, and advancing anchored shopping centers in Western Canada, has released information regarding a 1.7% decline in revenues. The revenue decreased from $1,143,880 in the quarter concluded on June 30, 2024, to $1,124,742 in the corresponding quarter this year.

data.title: MiniLuxe's financial report for 2nd quarter and 1st half of 2025 reveals double-digit sales growth, strong revenue, and 100% EBITDA growth for fleet.
data.description: Reports an impressive increase in system-wide sales, with double-digit growth. Additionally, noted a strong performance in same-store organic revenue. The company's fleet achieved remarkable 100% year-over-year growth in adjusted EBITDA.
Jerome Powell is scheduled to deliver his speech at the Jackson Hole Economic Policy Symposium at 10:00 a.m. Eastern Time (ET) today.

data.title: Crypto industry faces inflation problem due to unchecked token creation on Layer-2 networks. Ethereum criticized for devaluing tokens; competitors Solana, Celestia, and NEAR address the issue to enhance asset value and network stability. Ethereum criticized for token bloat, facing declining transaction activity, reduced user growth, and sliding revenues, leading to concerns over investment viability. Inflation in crypto occurs when token supply increases, devaluing existing tokens and destabilizing blockchains.
data.description: The article discusses the issue of "token bloat" in the blockchain space, focusing on Ethereum's Layer-2 networks allowing the issuance of tokens without considering transaction volumes, leading to inflation. This practice devalues tokens and destabilizes the blockchains. Ethereum transitioned to proof-of-stake in 2022 to combat deflation, but the problem has resurfaced, prompting competitors like Solana, Celestia, and NEAR to address it. Shrinking token supply is seen as a way to enhance asset value, improve network stability, and attract investors. Critics, like Crypto VC Nic Carter, criticize Ethereum for allowing unchecked token creation, leading to excessive token supply. This issue has led to concerns about Ethereum's investment potential due to declining transaction activity and sliding revenues. The article highlights the importance of addressing token bloat to maintain blockchain leadership.
Totalkredit A/S refinances floating rate loans, a move aimed at reducing interest costs for borrowers.

data.title: Frontline plc to release Q2 2025 results on Aug 29. Webcast and call at 3:00 p.m. CET, available at www.frontlineplc.cy.
data.description: Frontline plc. is set to announce its preliminary second quarter 2025 results on Friday, August 29, 2025. A webcast and conference call are scheduled to take place at 3:00 p.m. CET (9:00 a.m. U.S. Eastern Time) on that day. Those interested can download the results presentation beforehand from the Investor Relations section on www.frontlineplc.cy.
